title: Pair 07: FCFS vs Best-Fit Seating
summary: This comparison keeps the burger hall, reservation-heavy rush, and service pace fixed, but changes how open tables are assigned. Option A seats parties with the earliest suitable table, while Option B uses best-fit matching to test whether tighter table matching protects booked seats and improves flow when reservations and walk-ins overlap.

A.label: FCFS earliest suitable
A.notes: Hosts assign the earliest table that can fit each party while still protecting booked tables through the reservation grace window.

B.label: Best-fit table matching
B.notes: Hosts bias toward the tightest fitting open table so smaller reserved parties are less likely to consume flexible larger tables during the rush.
